mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in FEDFUNDS signals tighter policy and higher expected short rates, which lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices the path of policy and term premia upward.
failure_condition: If 20-day Treasury yield changes are not positive in triggered tightening periods, or if the sign accuracy of FEDFUNDS changes falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound, inflation expectations rise faster than policy rates, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next month as real yields and breakeven inflation reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger is met, the 10-year yield falls in a majority of 20-day windows or if CPIAUCSL surprises do not improve yield-direction hit rate relative to unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market slack and weaker wage pressure, which lowers expected inflation and growth, pulling the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day yield changes are not negative in the majority of UNRATE-rise episodes, or if the sign relationship reverses during repeated slackening episodes for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized expansion in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger household demand and payroll income, which lifts growth and inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-PAYEMS expansions fail to produce positive 20-day yield changes more often than not, or if either series alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness signals improving capacity utilization and stronger real activity, which increases cyclical inflation expectations and lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-decline INDPRO rebounds do not precede positive 20-day yield changes, or if the effect disappears when monthly INDPRO revisions are accounted for.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth combined with rising HOUST indicates a durable demand-led expansion, which raises expected real activity and inflation and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDP growth is positive and HOUST trends up for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the pair of GDP and housing strength does not improve 60-day yield-direction prediction versus GDP alone, or if yields do not trend higher across successive expansion bursts.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market stress regime, elevated UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS signals recession risk, which drives safe-haven demand for Treasuries and lowers the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 7% and PAYEMS declining for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are not negative during high-UNRATE payroll-contraction episodes, or if the signal loses predictive power once labor revisions are incorporated.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real rates become more negative and the market demands higher nominal compensation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-policy gaps do not precede higher 60-day yields, or if the relationship is absent when the same trigger is evaluated outside inflation-overshoot periods.
